speaker: kVmjBS7BmRr17sqzwVY7
text: Hello, and welcome back to Across the Pond & Down Under — the podcast where I, a rather damp and confused Englishman, try to make sense of the wild political and social landscape of Australia. Today we're talking about something making headlines right across the country — the 2026 Federal Budget, and what it actually means for older Australians. To help me understand, I'm joined by Peter from the Gold Coast. Peter, welcome to the show!
speech_text: Hello, and welcome back to Across the Pond and Down Under — the podcast where I, a rather damp and confused Englishman, try to make sense of the wild political and social landscape of Australia. Today we're talking about something making headlines right across the country — the 2026 Federal Budget, and what it actually means for older Australians. To help me understand, I'm joined by Peter from the Gold Coast. Peter, welcome to the show!

speaker: FAu9wa3gWCQ1SacKReJk
text: [sighs] Cheers, Oliver. Good to be here. I think.
speech_text: [sighs] Cheers, Oliver. Good to be here. I think.

speaker: kVmjBS7BmRr17sqzwVY7
text: Now Peter — the government just announced a massive aged care package. Billions of dollars. Thousands of new home care spots. Sounds rather positive, doesn't it?
speech_text: Now Peter — the government just announced a massive aged care package. Billions of dollars. Thousands of new home care spots. Sounds rather positive, doesn't it?

speaker: FAu9wa3gWCQ1SacKReJk
text: [laughs bitterly] Does it? Oliver, mate, with all due respect — you're dreaming. You're sitting over there drinking your weak tea, looking at a press release and thinking it's a victory lap. Tell you what — why don't you ask ME what it actually means on the ground?
speech_text: [laughs bitterly] Does it? Oliver, mate, with all due respect — you're dreaming. You're sitting over there drinking your weak tea, looking at a press release and thinking it's a victory lap. Tell you what — why don't you ask ME what it actually means on the ground?

speaker: kVmjBS7BmRr17sqzwVY7
text: ...Alright then. Peter, what does it actually mean on the ground?
speech_text: ...Alright then. Peter, what does it actually mean on the ground?

speaker: FAu9wa3gWCQ1SacKReJk
text: [sighs heavily] It means 347 days of waiting, Oliver. 266,000 of us stuck in limbo. THAT'S what it means.
speech_text: [sighs heavily] It means 347 days of waiting, Oliver. 266,000 of us stuck in limbo. THAT'S what it means.

speaker: kVmjBS7BmRr17sqzwVY7
text: [gasps] Good heavens. Nearly a year.
speech_text: [gasps] Good heavens. Nearly a year.

speaker: FAu9wa3gWCQ1SacKReJk
text: Nearly a year. And counting.
speech_text: Nearly a year. And counting.

speaker: kVmjBS7BmRr17sqzwVY7
text: But surely — 9,500 new home care packages must help? That's quite a significant number.
speech_text: But surely — 9,500 new home care packages must help? That's quite a significant number.

speaker: FAu9wa3gWCQ1SacKReJk
text: [laughs bitterly] Mate. There are 266,000 people on the waitlist. And they're giving us 9,500 spots. Do you know what that is? That's trying to put out a house fire with a water pistol.
speech_text: [laughs bitterly] Mate. There are 266,000 people on the waitlist. And they're giving us 9,500 spots. Do you know what that is? That's trying to put out a house fire with a water pistol.

speaker: kVmjBS7BmRr17sqzwVY7
text: I... yes, when you put it like that...
speech_text: I... yes, when you put it like that...

speaker: FAu9wa3gWCQ1SacKReJk
text: I'm 65, Oliver. I've worked hard, paid my taxes for forty years, and my knees sound like a pair of maracas when I walk down the stairs. [laughs] I just need a bit of support at home so I don't end up in a residential facility taking up a bed. Is that too much to ask?
speech_text: I'm 65, Oliver. I've worked hard, paid my taxes for forty years, and my knees sound like a pair of maracas when I walk down the stairs. [laughs] I just need a bit of support at home so I don't end up in a residential facility taking up a bed. Is that too much to ask?

speaker: kVmjBS7BmRr17sqzwVY7
text: Not at all, it sounds entirely reasonable—
speech_text: Not at all, it sounds entirely reasonable—

speaker: FAu9wa3gWCQ1SacKReJk
text: [excited] And as Senator Allman-Payne said — people are literally dying while they wait, Oliver. Dying. On. The. Waitlist.
speech_text: [excited] And as Senator Allman-Payne said — people are literally dying while they wait, Oliver. Dying. On. The. Waitlist.

speaker: kVmjBS7BmRr17sqzwVY7
text: [gasps] That's... that's quite sobering.
speech_text: [gasps] That's... that's quite sobering.

speaker: FAu9wa3gWCQ1SacKReJk
text: It's worse than sobering. It's a national disgrace. [sighs] They're rationing care, mate. Like it's a luxury item.
speech_text: It's worse than sobering. It's a national disgrace. [sighs] They're rationing care, mate. Like it's a luxury item.

speaker: kVmjBS7BmRr17sqzwVY7
text: What about the new co-payments? Asking wealthier older Australians to contribute a little for non-clinical services — help around the house, that sort of thing? Seems rather fair, doesn't it?
speech_text: What about the new co-payments? Asking wealthier older Australians to contribute a little for non-clinical services — help around the house, that sort of thing? Seems rather fair, doesn't it?

speaker: FAu9wa3gWCQ1SacKReJk
text: [laughs] Leave it to an economist to make getting help putting your socks on sound like a dodgy casino bet. [sighs] Every single person in this program has already been assessed by a clinician. We aren't ordering extra home care like it's a side of chips! We NEED it.
speech_text: [laughs] Leave it to an economist to make getting help putting your socks on sound like a dodgy casino bet. [sighs] Every single person in this program has already been assessed by a clinician. We aren't ordering extra home care like it's a side of chips! We NEED it.

speaker: kVmjBS7BmRr17sqzwVY7
text: But the public purse—
speech_text: But the public purse—

speaker: FAu9wa3gWCQ1SacKReJk
text: [excited] If they price people out of home care with these co-payments — people skip the care. Then they fall over. End up in the emergency department. Get pushed into residential aged care. Which costs the taxpayer a HELL of a lot more! It's penny wise and pound foolish!
speech_text: [excited] If they price people out of home care with these co-payments — people skip the care. Then they fall over. End up in the emergency department. Get pushed into residential aged care. Which costs the taxpayer a HELL of a lot more! It's penny wise and pound foolish!

speaker: kVmjBS7BmRr17sqzwVY7
text: So you're saying the short-term saving creates a much larger long-term cost.
speech_text: So you're saying the short-term saving creates a much larger long-term cost.

speaker: FAu9wa3gWCQ1SacKReJk
text: [sighs] Now you're getting it, mate. Took you a while, but you got there.
speech_text: [sighs] Now you're getting it, mate. Took you a while, but you got there.

speaker: kVmjBS7BmRr17sqzwVY7
text: So the frustration isn't just about money — it's about feeling abandoned by the system?
speech_text: So the frustration isn't just about money — it's about feeling abandoned by the system?

speaker: FAu9wa3gWCQ1SacKReJk
text: [excited] Exactly, mate! We're an easy target. Albo stands up there talking about seeing our people through the hard times — but it's all hot air. They promise you the world. And then you're 65, your private health costs are up, your investments are hit, and you're waiting nearly a year to get a bloke to come install a shower grab-rail.
speech_text: [excited] Exactly, mate! We're an easy target. Albo stands up there talking about seeing our people through the hard times — but it's all hot air. They promise you the world. And then you're 65, your private health costs are up, your investments are hit, and you're waiting nearly a year to get a bloke to come install a shower grab-rail.

speaker: kVmjBS7BmRr17sqzwVY7
text: A shower grab-rail. Not exactly asking for the moon, is it.
speech_text: A shower grab-rail. Not exactly asking for the moon, is it.

speaker: FAu9wa3gWCQ1SacKReJk
text: [laughs bitterly] Not the last time I checked, no.
speech_text: [laughs bitterly] Not the last time I checked, no.

speaker: kVmjBS7BmRr17sqzwVY7
text: [laughs] Well, look on the bright side, Peter — you do live on the beautiful Gold Coast! Endless sunshine, gorgeous beaches. Perhaps the sea air will sort everything out?
speech_text: [laughs] Well, look on the bright side, Peter — you do live on the beautiful Gold Coast! Endless sunshine, gorgeous beaches. Perhaps the sea air will sort everything out?

speaker: FAu9wa3gWCQ1SacKReJk
text: [laughs] Mate, I already eat Vegemite by the spoonful! But a view of the beach doesn't help when your knees are shot. The irony isn't lost on me. We live in paradise — yet my blood pressure only goes down when I'm at the pub having a cold one.
speech_text: [laughs] Mate, I already eat Vegemite by the spoonful! But a view of the beach doesn't help when your knees are shot. The irony isn't lost on me. We live in paradise — yet my blood pressure only goes down when I'm at the pub having a cold one.

speaker: kVmjBS7BmRr17sqzwVY7
text: I certainly hope the politicians in Canberra are listening to people like you, Peter.
speech_text: I certainly hope the politicians in Canberra are listening to people like you, Peter.

speaker: FAu9wa3gWCQ1SacKReJk
text: [sighs] They aren't listening, Oliver. They're just hoping we forget about it. Or expire while on the waitlist. [laughs bitterly] Whichever comes first.
speech_text: [sighs] They aren't listening, Oliver. They're just hoping we forget about it. Or expire while on the waitlist. [laughs bitterly] Whichever comes first.

speaker: kVmjBS7BmRr17sqzwVY7
text: Now Peter, before we wrap up — is there anything that gives you hope?
speech_text: Now Peter, before we wrap up — is there anything that gives you hope?

speaker: FAu9wa3gWCQ1SacKReJk
text: Hope? Oliver, I— [gasps] Hang on a sec, mate. That's my phone. Unknown number from Canberra... strewth! This could be it! This could be My Aged Care finally calling!
speech_text: Hope? Oliver, I— [gasps] Hang on a sec, mate. That's my phone. Unknown number from Canberra... strewth! This could be it! This could be My Aged Care finally calling!

speaker: kVmjBS7BmRr17sqzwVY7
text: [excited] Oh, brilliant! Live on the podcast! Go on, answer it!
speech_text: [excited] Oh, brilliant! Live on the podcast! Go on, answer it!

speaker: FAu9wa3gWCQ1SacKReJk
text: Hello? Yes, this is Peter... Yes, I am over 65... Yes, I have been waiting... Wait — what? [gasps] No, I don't need discounted solar panels! I don't even own a bloody roof in Canberra, I'm on the Coast! [sighs heavily]
speech_text: Hello? Yes, this is Peter... Yes, I am over 65... Yes, I have been waiting... Wait — what? [gasps] No, I don't need discounted solar panels! I don't even own a bloody roof in Canberra, I'm on the Coast! [sighs heavily]

speaker: kVmjBS7BmRr17sqzwVY7
text: ...Not the assessment team, then?
speech_text: ...Not the assessment team, then?

speaker: FAu9wa3gWCQ1SacKReJk
text: [laughs bitterly] Bloody telemarketer. I tell you what, Oliver — if they could harness the energy of my sheer rage right now, they wouldn't need to sell solar panels in Australia ever again.
speech_text: [laughs bitterly] Bloody telemarketer. I tell you what, Oliver — if they could harness the energy of my sheer rage right now, they wouldn't need to sell solar panels in Australia ever again.

speaker: kVmjBS7BmRr17sqzwVY7
text: [laughs] That is genuinely the most Australian thing I have ever heard. Peter, thank you so much for joining us. We're rooting for you.
speech_text: [laughs] That is genuinely the most Australian thing I have ever heard. Peter, thank you so much for joining us. We're rooting for you.

speaker: FAu9wa3gWCQ1SacKReJk
text: Cheers, Oliver. [sighs] I'll just go back to waiting. Only 346 days to go.
speech_text: Cheers, Oliver. [sighs] I'll just go back to waiting. Only 346 days to go.

speaker: kVmjBS7BmRr17sqzwVY7
text: Until next time, everyone. I'm Oliver — still confused, still learning. Goodbye!
speech_text: Until next time, everyone. I'm Oliver — still confused, still learning. Goodbye!

speaker: FAu9wa3gWCQ1SacKReJk
text: [laughs] She'll be right, mate. Probably.
speech_text: [laughs] She'll be right, mate. Probably.
